ab: Tanofakelohen a micepet ko edo, tanokaereden a micepet ko tikolacay nga’ matatodongay.
ch: A:老鼠應該要⽤石頭抓，⽵雞應該要⽤繩子抓，這樣才對。
en: It is correct to catch mice through rangay and catch bamboo partridges through qjiulang.

ab: Masahatiniay han, mararaway to kako! salaw cowa kapakacepecepet kako.
ch: B:原來是這樣，我都搞錯了！難怪我都抓不到。
en: No wonder! I am mistaken and that’s why I cannot catch any of them.

ab: Ano hatira haw i, pasifana’en ako kiso to samaanen a misanga’ kona tosa a talakal.
ch: A:那我再教你怎麼製作這兩種陷阱。
en: Then I will teach you how to set up these two traps.

ab: Hayda! tadafangcal! ano hatini haw i, ikor to wapakacepet to kako to ’a’adopen.
ch: B:好啊！太棒了！這樣我以後就會抓到獵物了。
en: Great! I will be able to catch the prey in the future.

ab: Sa akawang sanay to ko talod no omah, katayra ho kiso i soko a miala to kawkaw, talaomah kita.
ch: A:田裡的草太長了，你去倉庫拿鐮刀，我們去田裡。
en: The grass in the fields is too tall. Go to the warehouse to take the sickle and let’s go to the fields.

ab: i cowaay a mateli ko kawkaw?
ch: B:鐮刀放在哪裡?
en: Where are the sickles?

ab: i tatihiay no fakar, ano micomod kiso i soko i, ma’araw to.
ch: A:在籐籃的旁邊，你進去倉庫就會看到了。
en: It is next to the round basket. You will see it when you enter the warehouse.

ab: Kapina’on to a miala, ’arawi a madoka’. citalemay ko kawkaw.
ch: A:小心拿，不要受傷。鐮刀很利。
en: Be careful and don’t get hurt. The sickle is very sharp.

ab: Aka ripa’en ko talakal no mako.
ch: A:不要踩到我的陷阱。
en: Don’t step on my trap!

ab: I cowaay ko talakal no miso?
ch: B:你的陷阱在哪裡?
en: Where is your trap?

ab: itiraay i laeno nora kilang ko talakal no mako.
ch: A:我的陷阱在那棵樹的下面。
en: My trap is under that tree.

ab: mana pakaharateng ho kiso?
ch: B:你怎麼會記得?
en: How do you remember?

ab: iraay ko kacipinangan no mako itini, mafana’ay kako to i cowaay ko talakal ako.
ch: A:我在這邊有做記號，我知道我的陷阱在哪裡。
en: I make a mark here, so I know where my trap is.

ab: o fofo koni, telian niyam i ’alo a mifoting.
ch: A:這是魚筌，我們放在溪邊捕魚。
en: This is a fish trap which we put beside the river for fishing.

ab: awaay ko fofo no niyam itira, tanopacemotan no niyam ko pifoting.
ch: B:我們那邊沒有魚筌，我們都用釣竿抓魚。
en: We don’t have any fish traps there, so we catch fish with a fishing rod.

ab: cowa ho kapipacemot kako tono pacemot, manga’ay a pasifana’en kako ano cila haw?
ch: A:我沒有用過釣竿，你明天教我好嗎?
en: I have never used a fishing rod before, would you mind teaching me next time?

ab: hayda! malacafay kita a mipacemot ano cila.
ch: B:好啊!我們明天一起去釣魚。
en: Sure! Let’s go fishing together next time.

ab: pasifana’en ako kiso a misanga’ to fofo ano cila.
ch: A:那我明天教你做魚筌。
en: I will teach you how to make a fish trap next time.

ab: tatemikay to ko pacemot no miso, komaenay to ko foting to sapariri’ iso alatek! kalikien a mitengteng a patalafekang.
ch: A:你的釣竿動了，是不是有魚上鉤了!你快拉起來。
en: Your fishing rod is moving. Isn’t the fish on the hook? Pull it up!

ab: aka ho, macoyacoyay tato ko tamina. itini kako a mitengteng to pacemot, itira kiso a mipadang i takowanan a miala to salil a patayni.
ch: B:等一下，魚船太晃了。我在這邊拉釣竿，你去那邊幫我拿網子來。
en: Wait, the ship is rolling heavily. I am here to pull the fishing rod and you should go there to grab me the net.

ab: pataynien i takowanan a mitengteng ko foting, patadoen no mako kiso a patalafekang.
ch: A:你把魚拉到我這邊。我幫妳拉起來。
en: Pull the fish to my side and I will help you pull it up.

ab: tadafangcalay to makadofah ko kinaira ita anini, matomes ko paro no kanas.
ch: A:太好了!今天大豐收，魚簍都裝滿了。
en: Great! It is a bumper crop today with full fish baskets.

ab: kalikien no mita a minokay a mipalada’ to alomanay.
ch: B:我們快回去和大家分享。
en: Let’s go back immediately and share it with everyone.
